name: Coffee Meringue Kisses
recipeInstructions:
These cookies - said to have been a favorite with Marie Antoinette -
are 100 percent fat-free, and have about 20 calories per cookie. They
also have no flour, so would be good for someone who isn't able to
eat wheat. They will, however, tie up your oven for 3-4 hours.
1. Preheat the oven to 250°F (125°C). Use nonstick cookie sheets or line regular cookie sheets with parchment paper.
2. In a food processor with a metal blade, process the sugar and coffee powder until the texture of the sugar becomes very fine.
3. In a large mixing bowl, beat the egg whites and vanilla extract with an electric mixer at medium speed, until very frothy.
4. Add cream of tartar, and continue beating at medium speed until soft peaks form.
5. Increase beater speed to high, and gradually beat in sugar-coffee mixture. Continue beating until soft peaks form and the mixture has a glossy sheen.
6. To pipe cookies from pastry bag: Fill pastry bag (or 1-gallon freezer bag) fitted with Number 6 plain round pastry tube with meringue. Hold bag upright over prepared cookie sheets and squeeze out meringue, leaving pastry tip buried in meringue until you have piped a round about 1.5 inches in diameter. Lift up the tip and stop squeezing. Leave at least 2 inches between cookies.
7. Bake for 1 hour without opening the oven door. Turn off the oven, and let meringues dry in the oven for an additional 2-3 hours without opening the door. (After the first hour, it's okay to peek just once.) At this point you can leave them in the oven overnight.
8. Remove the meringues from the cookie sheet with a spatula. Store in an airtight container. Makes about 3 dozen.
